alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model demonstrates that a spiking recurrent network can effectively learn spatiotemporal dynamics on behaviorally relevant time scales.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a general result about learning spatiotemporal dynamics that subsumes the preprint's specific mechanism (encoding time then mapping to other dimensions); the mechanistic detail is not explicit in the pub claim, so the preprint detail is presented more generically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different spatiotemporal patterns can be learned and encoded through synaptic weights that follow common Hebbian learning rules.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model shows that different spatiotemporal patterns can be learned and encoded through synaptic weights following common Hebbian learning rules.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result directly with essentially the same wording and level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model is able to learn spatiotemporal dynamics on time scales that are behaviourally relevant.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model demonstrates that a spiking recurrent network can effectively learn spatiotemporal dynamics on behaviorally relevant time scales.
alignments.rationale: This is the same core result reported in the publication with matching scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned sequences are robustly replayed during a regime of spontaneous activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned sequences are robustly replayed during a regime of spontaneous activity.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains this claim verbatim, indicating no change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A method for training a car detection system using annotated data from a source domain without requiring annotations from the target domain is presented.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A method for training a car detection system using annotated data from a source domain without requiring image annotations from the target domain is presented.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with essentially identical wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to generate an artificial dataset with annotations for the target domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to generate an artificial dataset with annotations for the target domain.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The artificial dataset created by translating day-time images to night-time images significantly improved detection performance by more than 10% compared to training with only the available annotated data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrated that the proposed method achieved more than 10% improvement in detection performance compared to training with only the available annotated data.
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance improvement (more than 10%) is preserved in the publication; wording is slightly more general but meaning and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The original PINN method has been tested for its capability to represent complicated functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the work around Distributed Learning Machines and does not report the preprint's standalone statement about testing the original PINN's representation capability, so this specific claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed distributed PINN (DPINN) yields a more accurate solution to the Burgers' equation compared to the original PINN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DPINNs have potential advantages over existing PINNs for solving inverse problems in heterogeneous media.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserted a concrete accuracy improvement on Burgers' equation, while the publication softens and generalizes this to potential advantages of DPINNs for inverse problems — a broader but less definitive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DPINN is found to be more data-efficient than the original PINN when solving non-linear PDEs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a new physics-based interface regularization term to the cost function improves the performance of existing learning machines like the Physics Informed Neural Network (PINN) and the Physics Informed Extreme Learning Machine (PIELM).
alignments.rationale: The preprint's explicit claim about DPINN data-efficiency is not directly stated; the publication instead reports a different technical improvement (interface regularization) that improves performance of existing machines, which is related but weaker and differently framed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work represents the first attempt to directly solve the Navier-Stokes equation using a physics informed neural network.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert a first attempt on Navier–Stokes with PINNs; the focus and claims differ, so this specific novelty claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a novel approach for generating videos of the six basic facial expressions given a neutral face image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel approach for generating videos of the six basic facial expressions given a neutral face image.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in generating realistic videos with continuous motion, realistic appearance and identity preservation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in generating realistic videos with continuous motion, realistic appearance and identity preservation.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first work that explores manifold-valued representations with GAN to address the problem of dynamic facial expression generation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first work that explores manifold-valued representations with GAN to address the problem of dynamic facial expression generation.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains this novelty claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We show the efficiency of our framework for dynamic facial expressions generation, dynamic facial expression transfer and data augmentation for training improved emotion recognition models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We show the efficiency of our framework for dynamic facial expressions generation, dynamic facial expression transfer and data augmentation for training improved emotion recognition models.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this claim about applications and efficiency with similar confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Leo-III is an automated theorem prover for extensional type theory with Henkin semantics and choice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Leo-III is an automated theorem prover for extensional type theory with Henkin semantics and choice.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this primary contribution exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prover may cooperate with multiple external specialist reasoning systems such as first-order provers and SMT solvers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prover may cooperate with multiple external specialist reasoning systems such as first-order provers and SMT solvers.
alignments.rationale: The cooperation capability is preserved in the publication with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Leo-III supports reasoning in polymorphic first-order and higher-order logic, in all normal quantified modal logics, as well as in different deontic logics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Leo-III supports reasoning in polymorphic first-order and higher-order logic, in many quantified normal modal logics, as well as in different deontic logics.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens 'all' to 'many' quantified modal logics, narrowing the scope of the claim while keeping the overall capability claim and confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The development of Leo-III has initiated the ongoing extension of the TPTP infrastructure to reasoning within non-classical logics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Its development had initiated the ongoing extension of the TPTP infrastructure to reasoning within non-classical logics.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this contribution claim (minor tense change only) without change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mini-batch version of the Monte Carlo Markov Chain Stochastic Approximation Expectation-Maximization algorithm is proposed for general latent variable models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mini-batch version of the Monte Carlo Markov Chain Stochastic Approximation Expectation–Maximization algorithm is proposed for very large datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claimed target application from "general latent variable models" to a focus on "very large datasets," changing the claim's scope and thus constituting a semantic revision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm is shown to be convergent under classical conditions as the number of iterations increases for exponential models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm is shown to be convergent under classical conditions as the number of iterations increases for exponential models.
alignments.rationale: The convergence result is stated identically in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mini-batch sampling results in an important speed-up of the convergence of the sequence of estimators generated by the algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mini-batch sampling results in an important speed-up of the convergence of the sequence of estimators generated by the algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The speed-up claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Insights on the effect of the mini-batch size on the limit distribution are presented.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Insights on the effect of the mini-batch size on the limit distribution are presented.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about the mini-batch size effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mini-batch sampling can improve results when there is a constraint on computing time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mini-batch sampling can improve results when there is a constraint on computing time.
alignments.rationale: The practical conclusion about improvements under time constraints is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Considering the hierarchical structure of feature models may speed up convergence by 7.2% on average.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel exploration strategies using feature models can significantly improve the learning process in self-adaptive systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same idea that feature-model-based strategies improve learning but omits the specific 7.2% figure and frames the effect as a broader significant improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Considering the differences between feature models before and after an evolution step may speed up convergence by 64.6% on average.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results indicate an average speed-up of the learning process of 33.7% in the presence of many adaptation actions and 50.6% in the presence of evolution.
alignments.rationale: Both statements refer to speed-ups due to handling evolution, but the publication reports different empirical values (50.6% for evolution vs 64.6% in the preprint), indicating a revision and a slight tempering of effect size.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing online learning techniques randomly explore the possible adaptation actions, which can lead to slow convergence of the learning process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing solutions that randomly explore adaptation actions may exhibit slow learning when there are many possible adaptation actions.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the limitation but clarifies it applies particularly when many adaptation actions exist, narrowing the original general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing online learning techniques are unaware of changes in possible adaptation actions due to system evolution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel exploration strategies using feature models can significantly improve the learning process in self-adaptive systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the limitation verbatim but addresses it indirectly by proposing feature-model-based strategies that imply improved awareness of evolving actions; this reframes (clarifies) the original limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed feature graph learning algorithm demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in 3D point cloud denoising compared to competing schemes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying the feature graph learning algorithm to 3D point cloud denoising results in state-of-the-art performance compared to competing schemes.
alignments.rationale: The result about state-of-the-art performance on 3D point cloud denoising is preserved directly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm optimizes the diagonal and off-diagonal entries of a Mahalanobis distance matrix to compute an optimal feature graph.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed feature graph learning algorithm optimizes a feature metric to compute an optimal feature graph from a relevant feature vector per node.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core contribution (optimizing a feature metric for graph construction) but expresses it at a slightly higher-level (feature metric/vector) rather than explicitly calling out diagonal/off-diagonal Mahalanobis entries.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimization process constrains the Mahalanobis distance matrix to be positive definite using linear inequalities derived from the Gershgorin circle theorem.
alignments.rationale: The specific technical detail about using the Gershgorin circle theorem to impose positive-definiteness is not reflected in the publication claims and appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm mitigates the need for full eigen-decomposition of the Mahalanobis distance matrix, ensuring fast computation speed even with high-dimensional feature vectors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm mitigates the need for full eigen-decomposition of the Mahalanobis distance matrix, ensuring fast computation speed even with high-dimensional feature vectors.
alignments.rationale: This computational benefit is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.rationale: The performance comparison for occluded object recognition is equivalent between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The network’s representation of an occluded digit evolves over time due to recurrent connections.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recurrent connections tend to move the network's representation of an occluded digit towards its un-occluded version.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recurrent connections tend to move the network’s representation of an occluded digit towards its un-occluded version.
alignments.rationale: The mechanistic observation about recurrent connections is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Both the brain and artificial neural networks can exploit recurrent connectivity to aid occluded object recognition.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end approach segments hyperspectral images in a fully unsupervised way.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A new deep architecture that couples 3-D convolutional autoencoders with clustering is introduced.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental study demonstrates that the approach delivers high-quality segmentation without any prior class labels.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach not only predicts outfit compatibility but also diagnoses incompatible factors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach not only predicts outfit compatibility but also diagnoses incompatible factors.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and scope in preprint and publication; wording and meaning are the same.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new syntax with explicit negation can be captured by an extension of Equilibrium Logic.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial Neural Networks are universal approximators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial neural networks are universal approximators.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach can solve decision-making problems whose complexity exceeds the capabilities of individual decision-makers by combining them optimally. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model has direct applications in classification, regression, reinforcement learning, and adaptive control. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology allows a neural network to efficiently detect out-of-distribution (OOD) examples without significantly compromising classification accuracy on known classes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel loss function used in the Outlier Exposure with Confidence Control (OECC) method achieves superior results in OOD detection for both image and text classification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel loss function used in the Outlier Exposure with Confidence Control (OECC) method achieves superior results in OOD detection for both image and text classification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about OECC's superior OOD detection performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining OECC with state-of-the-art post-training OOD detection methods improves their performance in the OOD detection task. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining OECC with state-of-the-art post-training OOD detection methods improves their performance in the OOD detection task. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same experimental/result claim preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Histograms of edges - co-occurrences are not isotropic distributed and are strongly biased in horizontal and vertical directions of the stimulus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Geometrical properties of horizontal cortical connectivity can be considered as a neural correlate of a geometry of the visual plane.
alignments.rationale: The preprint reports a specific image-statistics observation (H/V bias) while the publication emphasizes a higher-level interpretation linking cortical connectivity geometry to the visual plane; the content is reinterpreted and generalized in the pub.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new model of non isotropic cortical connectivity is introduced, modeled on the histogram of edges - co-occurrences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new model of non isotropic cortical connectivity is introduced, modeled on statistics of images.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same contribution (a non-isotropic connectivity model) but frames the data source more generally as "statistics of images" rather than the specific histogram phrasing, clarifying and slightly broadening the formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced kernel justifies oblique phenomena comparable with experimental findings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new model justifies oblique phenomena comparable with experimental findings.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same conclusion that the introduced model/kernels account for oblique phenomena and compare to experimental findings with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The View Parsing Network (VPN) effectively parses first-view observations into a top-down-view semantic map at pixel-level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The View Parsing Network (VPN) effectively parses first-view observations into a top-down-view semantic map at pixel-level.
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical between preprint and publication in wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VPN is trained in a 3D graphics environment and utilizes domain adaptation to handle real-world data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VPN is trained in a 3D graphics environment and utilizes domain adaptation to handle real-world data.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated the same way in both versions with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the model can effectively utilize information from different views and multi-modalities to understand spatial information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that the model can effectively utilize information from different views and multi-modalities to understand spatial information.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same, but the publication lowers the expressed certainty ("demonstrate" -> "indicate"), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model enables surrounding sensing capability from 2D image input when implemented on a LoCoBot robot.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model enables surrounding sensing capability from 2D image input when implemented on a LoCoBot robot.
alignments.rationale: This experimental result is carried over unchanged into the publication with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep neural network predictions are vulnerable to universal adversarial perturbations that can fool a target network into making erroneous predictions.
alignments.rationale: This background/negative-claim about vulnerability is not restated as a standalone claim in the publication, which focuses on the defense contributions instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed defense operates in the DNN feature domain and effectively defends against universal perturbations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our novel defense operates in the DNN feature domain and effectively defends against universal adversarial perturbations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim about a feature-domain defense with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The defense outperforms existing strategies by more than 10% in restored accuracy across different network architectures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We outperform existing defense strategies across different network architectures by more than 10% in restored accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative performance claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The defense trained on ImageNet with one type of universal attack examples effectively defends against other types of unseen universal attacks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our defense trained on ImageNet with one type of universal attack examples effectively defends against other types of unseen universal attacks.
alignments.rationale: The generalization claim about training on one attack type and defending against unseen types is carried over with similar framing and medium certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Systematic improvements in diffractive optical neural networks were demonstrated using a differential measurement technique that mitigates the non-negativity constraint of light intensity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The differential measurement technique mitigates the strict nonnegativity constraint of light intensity in diffractive optical neural networks.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same technical advance (differential measurement mitigating the nonnegativity constraint) with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieved testing accuracies of 98.52%, 91.48%, and 50.82% for the MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, and grayscale CIFAR-10 datasets, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The class-specific differential detection in jointly optimized diffractive neural networks achieved blind testing accuracies of 98.52%, 91.48%, and 50.82% for MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, and grayscale CIFAR-10 datasets, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the identical accuracy numbers and attributes them to the class-specific differential detection scheme, matching the preprint's result claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification accuracies reported set the state-of-the-art for an all-optical neural network design.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The systematic advances in designing diffractive neural networks set the state of the art for all-optical neural network design.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim that these advances set the state of the art is preserved from preprint to publication with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By utilizing the inherent parallelization capability of optical systems, signal coupling between positive and negative detectors was reduced.
alignments.rationale: The specific phrasing about reduced signal coupling is not explicitly restated in the publication; related benefits may be subsumed under other contributions but the exact claim is absent.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the differential detection scheme, blind testing accuracies of 98.54%, 90.54%, and 48.51% were achieved for MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, and grayscale CIFAR-10 datasets, respectively.
alignments.rationale: This result (with slightly different accuracy numbers) appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint as a standalone claim, so it is an added published result.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented framework may provide low power solutions for various machine learning applications and aid in designing new computational cameras that are task-specific.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes this forward-looking contribution about applications and low-power designs that was not stated as a claim in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An adversary can optimally use its query budget for targeted evasion attacks against deep neural networks in a black-box setting.
alignments.rationale: No direct equivalent high-level claim about optimal budget use appears in the publication; related elements are present but this specific formulation is not restated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is an exploration-exploitation tradeoff in that query efficiency comes at the cost of effectiveness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is an exploration-exploitation tradeoff in that query efficiency comes at the cost of effectiveness.
alignments.rationale: The tradeoff claim is preserved; the publication frames it with higher certainty (publication-level phrasing is stronger), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two new attack strategies for using substitute models are as effective as previous query-only techniques but require significantly fewer queries, by up to three orders of magnitude.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two new attack strategies for using substitute models are as effective as previous 'query-only' techniques but require significantly fewer queries, by up to three orders of magnitude.
alignments.rationale: This result is carried over verbatim into the publication, preserving content, scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An agile adversary capable of switching through different attack techniques can achieve pareto-optimal efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An agile adversary capable of switching through different attack techniques can achieve pareto-optimal efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about agile adversaries and Pareto efficiency is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The difficulty of black-box attacks against real-world prediction APIs is significantly easier than previously thought, requiring approximately 500 queries instead of approximately 20,000 as in previous works.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The difficulty of targeted black-box attacks against real-world prediction APIs is significantly easier than previously thought, requiring approximately 500 queries instead of approximately 20,000 as in previous work.
alignments.rationale: This specific quantitative result about query counts is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Computational Prototype Model (CPM) is able to describe the internal semantic structure of objects categories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Computational Prototype Model (CPM) can represent the internal structure of semantic categories.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability of CPM to represent internal/category semantic structure with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The semantic distance metric can be understood as the object visual typicality score within a category.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert or discuss the semantic-distance-as-typicality interpretation, so this specific conceptual claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The descriptor encoding is semantically interpretable and significantly outperforms other image global encodings in clustering and classification tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on different image datasets demonstrated that the CPM approach may enhance machines' power of abstraction for semantic representation.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's strong performance and interpretability claims into a more general claim about enhancing abstraction, omitting explicit outperforming results and thus narrowing/tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nonparametric learning can make reliable predictions by extracting information from similarities between new input data and all samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nonparametric learning can make reliable predictions by extracting information from similarities between new input data and all samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core result verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A quantum paradigm of nonparametric learning offers an exponential speedup over the sample size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A quantum paradigm of nonparametric learning offers an exponential speedup over the sample size.
alignments.rationale: The claim about exponential speedup is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A quantum training state can be introduced to superpose all data of samples, encoding relevant information for learning in its bipartite entanglement spectrum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The power of quantum superposition for machine learning is demonstrated.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes demonstration of quantum superposition's power, which is a higher-level framing of the preprint's technical claim about using a superposed training state; the relation is plausible though the publication is less technical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A trained state for prediction can be obtained by entanglement spectrum transformation using quantum matrix toolbox.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A trained state for prediction can be obtained by entanglement spectrum transformation using the quantum matrix toolbox.
alignments.rationale: This technical method claim appears verbatim in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A feasible protocol to implement quantum nonparametric learning with trapped ions has been developed, demonstrating the power of quantum superposition for machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A feasible protocol to implement quantum nonparametric learning with trapped ions has been developed.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the protocol-development claim but states it with reduced certainty (medium); the demonstration-of-power wording is presented separately in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed 2.5D CNN outperforms its 2D and 3D counterparts in the segmentation of vestibular schwannoma tumors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our 2.5D CNN outperforms its 2D and 3D counterparts in vestibular schwannoma segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance comparison claim directly and with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The supervised attention mechanism outperforms unsupervised attention in the segmentation task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our supervised attention mechanism outperforms unsupervised attention in the segmentation task.
alignments.rationale: The supervised-vs-unsupervised attention result is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The voxel-level hardness-weighted Dice loss can improve the performance of CNNs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The voxel-level hardness-weighted Dice loss improves the segmentation accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same improvement from the hardness-weighted Dice loss.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieved an average Dice score and ASSD of 0.87 and 0.43 mm respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieved an average Dice score and ASSD of 0.87 and 0.43 mm respectively.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance figures are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BDNet model has achieved a test accuracy of 99.775% on the ISI Bengali handwritten numeral dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The BDNet model achieved a test accuracy of 99.78% on the ISI Bengali handwritten numeral dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same high test accuracy (minor rounding difference only), so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BDNet provides a 62.5% error reduction compared to previous state-of-the-art models for classifying Bengali handwritten digits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BDNet provides a 47.62% error reduction compared to previous state-of-the-art models.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a substantially different (smaller) error-reduction percentage, so the quantitative claim has been revised though the general comparative direction remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model was trained using untraditional data preprocessing and augmentation techniques to enhance its performance on different datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model was end-to-end trained using untraditional data preprocessing and augmentation techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the detail that training was end-to-end, clarifying the original training-description without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A dataset of 1000 images of Bengali handwritten numerals was created to test the trained model, yielding promising results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A dataset of 1000 images of Bengali handwritten numerals was created to test the trained model.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report creation of the 1000-image dataset; the publication omits the informal "promising" qualifier but the factual dataset claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Deep Spatio-Temporal neural Networks (DSTNs) improve the click-through rate (CTR) prediction by effectively utilizing auxiliary ads from both spatial and temporal domains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Deep Spatio-Temporal neural Networks (DSTNs) improve click-through rate (CTR) prediction by effectively utilizing auxiliary ads from both spatial and temporal domains.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core contribution unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DSTNs outperform several state-of-the-art methods for CTR prediction based on offline experiments conducted on one public dataset and two industrial datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DSTNs outperform several state-of-the-art methods for CTR prediction based on offline experiments conducted on one public dataset and two industrial datasets.
alignments.rationale: The offline experimental comparison claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deployment of the best-performing DSTN in Shenma Search resulted in a significant improvement in online CTR compared to the previous serving model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deployment of the best-performing DSTN in Shenma Search resulted in a significant improvement in online CTR compared to the previous serving model.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same online-deployment benefit with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned conjugate gradient descent network (LcgNet) reduces the computational complexity of signal detection in massive MIMO systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned conjugate gradient descent network (LcgNet) reduces the complexity of signal detection in massive MIMO systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim with nearly identical meaning and emphasis on complexity reduction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed LcgNet achieves promising performance compared to conventional MIMO systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed network achieves promising performance with much lower complexity compared to conventional MIMO systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit emphasis on much lower complexity while preserving the original performance claim, a clarifying refinement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel quantized LcgNet is introduced to reduce memory costs by integrating a low-resolution nonuniform quantizer. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel quantized LcgNet is introduced, which uses a low-resolution nonuniform quantizer to reduce memory costs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent wording and intent about quantization and memory reduction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed networks are easy and fast to train due to the limited number of learnable parameters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of learnable parameters in the proposed networks is limited, making them relatively easy to train. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about limited parameters enabling relatively easy training with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We develop a time-series anomaly detection service that helps customers monitor time-series continuously and alert for potential incidents. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We develop a time-series anomaly detection service that helps customers monitor time-series continuously and alert for potential incidents. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the service development claim verbatim; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a novel algorithm based on Spectral Residual (SR) and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for time-series anomaly detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel algorithm based on Spectral Residual (SR) and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for time-series anomaly detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The algorithm claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach achieves superior experimental results compared with state-of-the-art baselines on both public datasets and Microsoft production data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach achieves superior experimental results compared with state-of-the-art baselines on both public datasets and Microsoft production data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Results and evaluation claims are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work is the first attempt to borrow the SR model from the visual saliency detection domain to time-series anomaly detection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work is the first attempt to borrow the SR model from the visual saliency detection domain to time-series anomaly detection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Population-level metrics, such as AUC-PR, AUC-ROC, log-likelihood, and calibration error, do not capture model uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Population-level metrics, such as AUC-PR, AUC-ROC, log-likelihood, and calibration error, do not capture model uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same observation about population-level metrics and uncertainty without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is significant variability in patient-specific predictions and optimal decisions, which motivates the need for capturing model uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is significant variability in patient-specific predictions and optimal decisions, which motivates the need for capturing model uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about patient-level variability motivating uncertainty capture.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RNNs with only Bayesian embeddings can be a more efficient way to capture model uncertainty compared to ensembles. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RNNs with only Bayesian embeddings can be a more efficient way to capture model uncertainty compared to ensembles. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The methodological efficiency claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding the uncertainty for individual patients has clear clinical impact, such as determining when a model decision is likely to be brittle. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Understanding the uncertainty for individual patients has clear clinical impact, such as determining when a model decision is likely to be brittle. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The clinical-impact conclusion remains the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach predicts several samples of the future using a winner-takes-all loss and iterative grouping of samples to multiple modes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method involves the prediction of several samples of the future with a winner-takes-all loss and iterative grouping of samples to multiple modes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the same methodological contribution with equivalent components and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach effectively evaluates predicted multimodal distributions even when only a single sample from the ground-truth distribution is available for evaluation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation method discussed is applicable in scenarios where only a single sample from the ground-truth distribution is available. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements claim applicability of the evaluation approach in single-sample ground-truth scenarios with the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates good estimates of multimodal distributions and successfully avoids mode collapse. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach triggers good estimates of multimodal distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the good estimation of multimodal distributions; the preprint additionally claims explicit avoidance of mode collapse, so the publication partly reframes that result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm achieves considerable regret reduction in online influence maximization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm achieves considerable regret reduction in online influence maximization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same strong result claim about regret reduction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a factorization based online learning solution that estimates latent factors to improve activation probabilities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The factorization of activation probability into latent factors improves the estimation of activation probabilities. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as a conclusion about improved estimation; content is the same but presented with slightly different emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive empirical evaluations on two real-world networks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed solution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed solution demonstrates effectiveness in empirical evaluations on two real-world networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical-evaluation result is preserved with equivalent meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The influence maximization problem is significantly impacted by network assortativity, which is often overlooked in existing works. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Network assortativity is an important property that is often ignored in existing works on online influence maximization. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both claim assortativity is important and overlooked; the publication slightly reframes it as an overlooked property, so this is a minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BIDIFAC (Bidimensional Integrative Factorization) is proposed for integrative dimension reduction and signal approximation of bidimensionally linked data matrices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, bidimensional integrative factorization (BIDIFAC), facilitates integrative dimension reduction and signal approximation of bidimensionally linked data matrices.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with essentially identical wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method factorizes the data into globally shared, row-shared, column-shared, and single-matrix structural components.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BIDIFAC factorizes data into globally shared, row-shared, column-shared, and single-matrix structural components.
alignments.rationale: The structural decomposition claim is carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BIDIFAC facilitates the investigation of shared and unique patterns of variability in biomedical data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, bidimensional integrative factorization (BIDIFAC), facilitates integrative dimension reduction and signal approximation of bidimensionally linked data matrices.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes general integrative dimension reduction for bidimensional matrices rather than the preprint's biomedical-focused phrasing, broadening the scope and clarifying the general framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The estimation in BIDIFAC uses a penalized objective function that extends the nuclear norm penalization for a single matrix.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method uses a penalized objective function that extends the nuclear norm penalization for a single matrix.
alignments.rationale: The core methodological claim is the same, but the publication reports a lower certainty level (medium), indicating a tempered presentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BIDIFAC was applied to integrate two genomics platforms across two sample cohorts using breast cancer data from TCGA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BIDIFAC was applied to integrate two genomics platforms across two sample cohorts using breast cancer data from the Cancer Genome Atlas.
alignments.rationale: The application to TCGA breast cancer data is described the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct genomic subtypes of lower-grade glioma tumors are associated with shape features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct genomic subtypes of lower-grade glioma tumors are associated with shape features.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The bounding ellipsoid volume ratio shows a strong association with RNASeq clusters ($p<0.0002$).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The bounding ellipsoid volume ratio shows a strong association with RNASeq clusters (p < 0.0002).
alignments.rationale: The specific statistical association and p-value are preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm used for tumor segmentation achieved a mean Dice coefficient of 82%, comparable to human performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm used for tumor segmentation achieved a mean Dice coefficient of 82%, comparable to human performance.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is reproduced unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Associations were identified between the bounding ellipsoid volume ratio and all tested molecular subtypes ($p<0.02$).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Associations were identified between the bounding ellipsoid volume ratio and all tested molecular subtypes (p < 0.02).
alignments.rationale: The reported associations and p-values are carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Wasserstein distance provides a more desirable optimisation space compared to the Kullback-Leibler divergence for estimating elliptical mixture models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Wasserstein distance provides a more desirable optimisation space compared to the Kullback–Leibler divergence for estimating elliptical mixture models.
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion comparing Wasserstein and KL divergences is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method offers a stable solution to elliptical mixture models that is robust to initialisations and reaches a superior optimum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed optimisation method on a statistical manifold defined under an approximate Wasserstein distance significantly stabilises and improves the estimation of elliptical mixture models.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains claims of stabilization and improvement but omits explicit statements about robustness to initialisation and achieving a superior optimum, representing a toned-down/revised claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An efficient optimisation method on a statistical manifold defined under an approximate Wasserstein distance significantly stabilises and improves the estimation of elliptical mixture models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed optimisation method on a statistical manifold defined under an approximate Wasserstein distance significantly stabilises and improves the estimation of elliptical mixture models.
alignments.rationale: This methodological claim is directly matched and retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the excellent performance of the proposed elliptical mixture model solver.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the excellent performance of the proposed elliptical mixture model solver.
alignments.rationale: The empirical performance claim is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Amur Tiger Re-identification in the Wild (ATRW) dataset contains over 8,000 video clips from 92 Amur tigers, with bounding box, pose keypoint, and tiger identity annotations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Amur Tiger Re-identification in the Wild (ATRW) dataset contains over 8,000 video clips from 92 Amur tigers, with bounding box, pose keypoint, and tiger identity annotations.
alignments.rationale: The dataset description is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ATRW is a challenging dataset for re-identification, as demonstrated with a set of baseline algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ATRW is a challenging dataset for re-identification, as demonstrated with a set of baseline algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation and challenge claim carries over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for tiger re-identification introduces precise pose parts modeling in deep neural networks and achieves notable performance improvement over existing re-ID methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for tiger re-identification introduces precise pose parts modeling in deep neural networks and achieves notable performance improvement over existing re-ID methods.
alignments.rationale: The methodological and performance claim is reproduced exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The semi-supervised manifold alignment (SSMA) algorithm amplifies the impact of an existing training set by linking labeled data to unlabeled data via unsupervised techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MAPPER-induced manifold alignment (MIMA) unites SSMA with MAPPER to enhance the semi-supervised fusion of multi-sensory data sources.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's general statement about SSMA's effect is reframed in the publication as part of the MIMA construction that enhances semi-supervised fusion, clarifying the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first time that SSMA has been applied on fusing optical data and SAR data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ReLU FNNs with width $\text{O}(\text{max}\{d\lfloor N^{1/d}\rfloor, N+1\})$ and depth $\text{O}(L)$ can approximate an arbitrary H"older continuous function of order $\eta\in (0,1]$ on $[0,1]^d$ with a nearly tight approximation rate $\text{O}(\sqrt{d} N^{-2\eta/d}L^{-2\eta/d})$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ReLU FNNs with width $\text{O}(\text{max}\{d\lfloor N^{1/d}\rfloor,N+1\})$ and depth $\text{O}(L)$ can approximate an arbitrary Hölder continuous function of order $α∈(0,1]$ on $[0,1]^d$ with a nearly tight approximation rate $\text{O}(\sqrt{d}N^{−2α/d}L^{−2α/d})$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same approximation result; only notation for the Hölder exponent (η → α) differs, not the meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For an arbitrary continuous function $f$ on $[0,1]^d$ with a modulus of continuity $\omega_f(\cdot)$, the constructive approximation rate is $\text{O}(\sqrt{d}\,\omega_f(N^{-2/d}L^{-2/d})).$ (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The constructive approximation rate for an arbitrary continuous function $f$ on $[0,1]^d$ with a modulus of continuity $ω_f (·)$ is $\text{O}(\sqrt{d}\,ω_f(N^{−2α/d}L^{−2α/d})).$ (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The constructive rate is the same; the publication uses α notation and consistent exponent placement but preserves the mathematical content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the case of an essentially low-dimensional domain, the approximation rate for ReLU FNNs to approximate $f$ in the $\epsilon$-neighborhood is $\text{O}(\omega_f(\frac{\epsilon}{1-\delta}\sqrt{\frac{d}{d_\delta}}+\epsilon)+\sqrt{d}\,\omega_f(\frac{\sqrt{d}}{(1-\delta)\sqrt{d_\delta}}N^{-2/d_\delta}L^{-2/d_\delta})).$ (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the case of an essentially low-dimensional domain, the approximation rate for ReLU FNNs to approximate $f$ in the $\varepsilon$-neighborhood is $\text{O}(ω_f(\frac{ε}{1−δ}\sqrt{\frac{d}{d_δ}}+ε)+\sqrt{d}\,ω_f(\frac{\sqrt{d}}{1−δ\sqrt{d_δ}}N^{−2α/d_δ}L^{−2α/d_δ})).$ (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same low-dimensional approximation bound with slight re-formatting and notation adjustments (α for exponent); the mathematical content is preserved but presentation is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conventional CNNs trained with only the spectral loss produce PS images of low visual quality, including double-edge artifacts and ghosting artifacts. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed spectral-spatial structure (S3) loss effectively improves pan-sharpening results. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Selective prediction-set models trained using an uncertainty-aware loss minimization framework provide more reliable predictions than black-box predictors. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method allows for the calculation of point estimates and confidence intervals for the true coverage of selective prediction-set models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SPS ensembles attain prediction-set coverage rates closer to the nominal level and have narrower confidence intervals for its marginal coverage rate. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When applied to predicting in-hospital mortality and length-of-stay for ICU patients, our model outperforms existing approaches on both in-sample and out-of-sample age groups. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: SPS ensembles attain prediction-set coverage rates closer to the nominal level and have narrower confidence intervals for its marginal coverage rate. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method allows for computationally efficient approximations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim about computational efficiency is unchanged in wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method is equivalent to constrained Bayesian variational inference. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method is equivalent to constrained Bayesian variational inference. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Equivalence statement is identical across versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates superiority over existing approaches in fast log determinant estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates superiority over existing approaches in fast log determinant estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance-superiority claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates superiority over existing approaches in information-theoretic Bayesian optimisation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates superiority over existing approaches in information-theoretic Bayesian optimisation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim asserting superiority in Bayesian optimisation is the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed parameter pruning (PP) technique removes redundant channels in a neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of parameter pruning, parameter quantization, and feature-map quantization can produce a compacted speech enhancement model with a size of only 9.76% compared to that of the original model.
alignments.rationale: The publication still includes parameter pruning but reframes it as part of an integrated set of techniques (adds feature-map quantization) rather than the standalone claim of removing redundant channels, changing the semantic focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parameter quantization (PQ) technique reduces the size of a neural network by representing weights with fewer cluster centroids.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of parameter pruning, parameter quantization, and feature-map quantization can produce a compacted speech enhancement model with a size of only 9.76% compared to that of the original model.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains parameter quantization but primarily reports it within the integrated methods and a final size result, clarifying its role in the combined approach rather than changing the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of PP and PQ techniques produces a compacted speech enhancement model with a size of only 10.03% compared to the original model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of parameter pruning, parameter quantization, and feature-map quantization can produce a compacted speech enhancement model with a size of only 9.76% compared to that of the original model.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different (smaller) final size and explicitly adds feature-map quantization to the integration, altering the numerical result and the set of techniques considered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The compacted model results in minor performance losses of 1.43% for STOI and 3.24% for PESQ.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The compacted model results in minor performance losses of 0.01 for STOI scores and 0.03 for PESQ scores.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report minor performance losses, but the reported loss magnitudes differ substantially between preprint and publication, representing a revision of the quantitative results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest that the PP and PQ techniques can be used in a speech enhancement system in devices with limited storage and computation resources.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed techniques can effectively reduce the storage of a speech enhancement system on edge devices.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the conclusion to the proposed techniques (including feature-map quantization) and states the applicability to edge devices with a higher reported certainty (medium vs low), amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide a large image parameter dataset extracted from the Solar Dynamics Observatory (SDO) mission's AIA instrument, with a volume of just short of 1 TiB per year.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide a large image parameter data set extracted from the Solar Dynamics Observatory (SDO) mission’s Atmospheric Imaging Assembly (AIA) instrument.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim restates the same contribution of providing a large AIA image-parameter dataset with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We improve upon the performance of a set of ten image parameters for region classification of active regions and coronal holes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We improve on the performance of a set of 10 image parameters for the region classification of active regions and coronal holes.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim verbatim in meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Utilizing JP2 images, which are significantly smaller files, is not detrimental to the region classification task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Utilizing JP2 images, which are significantly smaller files, is not detrimental to the region classification task.
alignments.rationale: Both documents state the same conclusion about JP2 images' suitability for the classification task.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide a public API to access the dataset, which can be used in a range of studies on AIA images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data set can be used in a range of studies on AIA images, such as content-based image retrieval or tracking of solar events.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that the data set can be used in a range of studies but omits explicit mention of providing a public API, effectively revising the contribution description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analyzing speech and language production provides insights into an individual's cognitive ability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analyzing speech and language production provides insights into an individual's cognitive ability.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advances in speech signal processing and natural language processing have led to the development of tools for detecting subtle changes in cognitive-linguistic function.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The review categorizes speech and language features into language features based on natural language processing and speech features based on speech signal processing.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The features discussed aim to measure dimensions of cognitive-linguistics such as language diversity, syntactic complexity, semantic coherence, and timing.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper proposes new research directions to advance the field of cognitive-linguistic assessment.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) can be effectively used to classify human activities based on raw data from inertial sensors.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Different network architectures can adapt motion signals for input into CNNs, enhancing classification performance.
alignments.rationale: The conceptual claim about network architectures and adaptation is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification potential of human activities varies when using single, double, and triple sensor systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification potential of human activities varies when using single, double, and triple sensor systems.
alignments.rationale: The comparative claim regarding sensor configurations is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results on a dataset of 16 lower-limb activities indicate promising performance of the proposed models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results on a dataset of 16 lower-limb activities indicate promising performance of the proposed models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Noisy spiking networks are more robust than those without noise and leakage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spiking neural networks utilizing biological non-idealities show higher robustness under weight perturbations.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same robustness finding but rephrases it in terms of biological non-idealities and weight perturbations, clarifying context without changing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A noisy network can tolerate the inaccuracy expected when hafnium-oxide based resistive random-access memory is used to encode synaptic weights.
alignments.pub_claim_text: One of the evolved spiking networks can perform well under the variance expected when using hafnium-oxide based resistive memory to encode synaptic weights.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Information Distilling of Metastability (IDM) method is end-to-end differentiable and scalable to ultra-large datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: IDM is an end-to-end differentiable method that is scalable to ultralarge data sets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IDM is capable of clustering samples while simultaneously reducing dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: With very robust networks, adversarial attacks may be leveraged as explanations with greater fidelity to the model. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network information converter can transform input information from digital to analogue and perform information processing after training. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Challenges such as noisy computations and limited precision of analog-to-digital converters will significantly impact the training of state-of-the-art ANNs.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model matches or outperforms single-task counterparts in person re-identification, attribute classification, body part segmentation, and pose estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Certain authorship verification methods can attain over 75% accuracy when verifying scientific documents written at different times with an average difference of 15.6 years.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Certain authorship verification methods can attain over 75% accuracy when verifying scientific documents written at different times with an average difference of 15.6 years.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same measured result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All examined authorship verification methods are prone to errors in cross-topic verification cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All examined authorship verification methods are prone to challenges in cross-topic verification cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases 'errors' as 'challenges' but retains the same limitation claim about cross-topic performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adversarial perturbations can trick CNN-based hotspot detectors into predicting modified layouts as hotspot free with up to 99.7% success.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adversarial perturbations can trick CNN-based hotspot detectors into predicting modified layouts as hotspot free with up to 99.7% success.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same quantitative attack success result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Small, functionality preserving, and design-constraint satisfying changes to a layout can lead to incorrect predictions by CNN-based hotspot detectors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Small, functionality preserving, and design-constraint-satisfying changes to a layout can still lead to incorrect predictions by CNN-based hotspot detectors.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same, but the publication marks this as a conclusion with lower reported certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An adversarial retraining strategy significantly improves the robustness of CNN-based hotspot detection against adversarial attacks by a factor of approximately 3 without compromising classification accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An adversarial retraining strategy significantly improves the robustness of CNN-based hotspot detection by a factor of ~3 against adversarial attacks.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the improvement-by-~3 claim but omits the explicit 'without compromising classification accuracy' clause present in the preprint, which is a slight clarification/reduction in claimed benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of soft computing methods effectively addresses the problem of locating garbage accumulation points in urban scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of soft computing methods effectively addresses the problem of locating garbage accumulation points in urban scenarios.
alignments.rationale: Result claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental evaluation on real scenarios in Montevideo and Bahia Blanca demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental evaluation on real scenarios in Montevideo and Bahía Blanca demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed approaches.
alignments.rationale: Same experimental-evidence claim; only minor diacritical spelling change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods allow for planning with different trade-offs between the objectives of waste management.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods allow for planning with different trade-offs between the objectives of waste management.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about trade-offs is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computed results improve over the current planning in Montevideo and provide a reasonable budget cost and quality of service for Bahia Blanca.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computed results improve over the current planning in Montevideo and provide a reasonable budget cost and quality of service for Bahía Blanca.
alignments.rationale: Result about improvements and reasonable budget/quality is preserved in the publication (minor spelling change only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DNN testing method based on conditioning on the representation learned by the DNN model improves efficiency in operational testing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed DNN testing method based on conditioning on the representation learned by the DNN model improves efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The publication shortens the phrasing by removing the specific phrase "operational testing," slightly broadening the scope while preserving the original meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm designed for sampling from the high dimensional distribution of DNN outputs leverages cross entropy minimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new algorithm designed for sampling from the high dimensional distribution leverages cross entropy minimization.
alignments.rationale: Wording and content are effectively identical between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compared with simple random sampling, the proposed approach requires only about half of the labeled inputs to achieve the same level of precision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach requires only about half of the labeled inputs compared to simple random sampling to achieve the same level of precision.
alignments.rationale: The numerical efficiency result is restated verbatim in the publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian inference allows for reliable fitting of the two-compartment exchange model to perfusion data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A Bayesian inference scheme allows for the reliable estimation of parameters in a two-compartment exchange model from myocardial perfusion MR data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core claim about reliable parameter estimation with Bayesian inference in the same context, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of prior knowledge on kinetic parameters and the similarity of neighbouring voxels significantly improves the reliability of perfusion estimates compared to traditional least-squares fitting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian inference approach results in a significant reduction in mean-squared error compared to standard non-linear least squares fitting.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the improvement in terms of a quantified MSE reduction rather than the more general "reliability," making the claim more specific but consistent with the original intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average normalized mean square error for the simulation phantom decreases from 0.32 to 0.13 when using Bayesian inference instead of least-squares fitting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian inference approach results in a significant reduction in mean-squared error compared to standard non-linear least squares fitting.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a summarized statement about MSE reduction but omits the specific numeric example given in the preprint; this is a more general restatement of the same result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The assessment of coronary artery disease based on quantitative MBF maps obtained using Bayesian inference matches visual assessment in all 24 slices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parameter maps generated by the Bayesian inference scheme align with independent clinical diagnoses of the patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication relaxes the strong, specific preprint statement (exact match in all 24 slices) into a more general alignment with clinical diagnoses and presents it with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only 16 out of 24 slices achieve a corresponding assessment of coronary artery disease when using maps obtained by least-squares fitting.
alignments.rationale: The specific comparative slice-level result for least-squares fitting is not present in the publication and thus appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Context-Aware Visual Policy network (CAVP) improves fine-grained image-to-language generation for both image sentence and paragraph captioning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Context-Aware Visual Policy network (CAVP) improves fine-grained image-to-language generation for both image sentence and paragraph captioning.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is restated verbatim in the publication, preserving meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAVP explicitly considers previous visual attentions as context, enhancing the generation of current words and sentences based on current visual attention.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAVP explicitly considers previous visual attentions as context, enhancing the generation of current words and sentences based on current visual attention.
alignments.rationale: The content is preserved but the publication reports this claim with reduced certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered while the semantic content remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAVP outperforms traditional visual attention mechanisms by allowing attention to complex visual compositions over time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAVP outperforms traditional visual attention mechanisms by allowing attention to complex visual compositions over time.
