alignments.preprint_claim_text: The image captioning model, including CAVP and its language policy network, can be optimized end-to-end using an actor-critic policy gradient method.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the specific claim about end-to-end optimization with actor-critic policy gradient, and instead emphasizes empirical benchmark results, so the methodological optimization claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The incorporation of knowledge about semantic attributes improves the accuracy of all ConvNet architectures studied.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of knowledge about semantic attributes improves the accuracy of all ConvNet architectures studied.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the finding directly, preserving the claim's content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Exploring only images related to the context of the study, outdoor in this case, is recommended, as indoor images were not significantly helpful.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Exploring only images related to the context of the study—outdoor images—is recommended, as indoor images were not significantly helpful.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation about focusing on context-relevant (outdoor) images is retained with similar wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate applicability in the city of Chicago, helping to improve the knowledge of subjective characteristics of different areas of the city.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate applicability in the city of Chicago, Illinois, indicating that they can enhance understanding of subjective characteristics of different areas.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines phrasing (adds the state and slightly alters wording) but preserves the substantive claim about applicability to Chicago and understanding subjective characteristics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Certain areas of the city tend to concentrate more images of a specific class of sentiment, which are correlated with median income.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Certain areas of the city tend to concentrate more images of a specific class of sentiment, which are correlated with median income.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DRL-MOA framework effectively solves multi-objective optimization problems by decomposing them into scalar optimization subproblems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DRL-based multiobjective optimization algorithm (DRL-MOA) effectively solves multiobjective optimization problems (MOPs) using deep reinforcement learning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates strong generalization ability and fast solving speed compared to existing methods for multiobjective optimizations.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DIG (Dynamical Information Geometry) is a visualization method for multivariate time series data that extracts an information geometry from a diffusion framework. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel group of distances implemented in DIG may reveal structure in the data that is not accessible by commonly used diffusion distances. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A case study applying the visualization tool to EEG data demonstrates its effectiveness in visualizing sleep stages. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The enhancement in memory capacity is attributed to the generation of a Psuedo-inverse correlation in the connectivity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting connectivity from the learning process decorrelates the target patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the mechanistic outcome differently (decorrelation of target patterns) rather than explicitly claiming the generation of a pseudo-inverse correlation; the mechanism wording has been altered while the general effect is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spontaneous activity intermittently exhibits neural patterns corresponding to embedded memories. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spontaneous activity emerges that intermittently exhibits neural patterns corresponding to embedded memories. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a small phrasing change ('emerges that') but preserves the same phenomenon and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stabilization of memories is achieved by a distinct bifurcation from the spontaneous activity under the application of each input. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stabilization of memories is achieved by a distinct bifurcation from the spontaneous activity under the application of each input. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim text is identical but the published certainty is lower (medium vs high), indicating a tempering of confidence while keeping the claim content the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed nearest neighbour method improves the implementation of Isolation Similarity compared to a tree method.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a claim about improving the implementation of Isolation Similarity versus a tree method; that specific implementation-comparison claim is absent in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The clustering performance of the classic density-based clustering algorithm DBSCAN can be significantly uplifted to surpass that of the recent density-peak clustering algorithm DP by using the proposed nearest-neighbour-induced Isolation Similarity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clustering performance of the classic density-based clustering algorithm DBSCAN can be significantly uplifted to surpass that of the recent density-peak clustering algorithm DP by replacing the distance measure with the proposed nearest-neighbour-induced Isolation Similarity.
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication (DBSCAN uplift via replacing distance with proposed similarity).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new type of clusters called mass-connected clusters is formally defined, which DBSCAN detects when the distance measure is replaced with the proposed similarity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new type of clusters called mass-connected clusters is formally defined.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly includes the formal definition of mass-connected clusters stated in the preprint; the detection aspect is also present elsewhere in the paper but this claim directly matches the formal-definition sentence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conditions under which mass-connected clusters can be detected while density-connected clusters cannot are provided.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conditions under which mass-connected clusters can be detected while density-connected clusters cannot are provided.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conditional results with similar certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed paradigm replaces traditional image registration with a conditional segmentation algorithm that learns the location of corresponding image-specific ROIs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed paradigm replaces traditional image registration with a conditional segmentation algorithm that learns the location of corresponding image-specific ROIs.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is carried over verbatim to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The median target registration error (TRE) achieved using the proposed method is 2.1 mm between the ground-truth ROIs defined on intraoperative ultrasound images and those propagated from preoperative MR images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The median target registration error (TRE) achieved using the proposed method is 2.1 mm between ground-truth ROIs on intraoperative ultrasound images and those propagated from preoperative MR images.
alignments.rationale: The numeric result and its context are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed conditional segmentation method results in significantly lower TREs, over 34% improvement, compared to a previously-proposed spatial-transformation-predicting registration network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed conditional segmentation method results in significantly lower TREs, over 34% improvement, compared to a previously-proposed spatial-transformation-predicting registration network.
alignments.rationale: The comparative improvement claim is retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A quantitative bias-variance analysis was used to explain the observed improvement in registration accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A quantitative bias-variance analysis provides an explanation for the observed improvement in registration accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The explanatory analysis claim is present with equivalent meaning in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm minimizes an empirically chosen cost function over a set of parameters for routing Goldstone diagrams.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm minimizes an empirically chosen cost function over a set of parameters for routing Goldstone diagrams.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution about the cost-function-based routing algorithm is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The routing problem is well defined and fully discrete in terms of the cost function, discrete vertex placement, and interchange of particle/hole lines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The routing problem is well defined and fully discrete in terms of the cost function, discrete vertex placement, and interchange of particle/hole lines.
alignments.rationale: The characterization of the routing problem remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm demonstrates an exponential complexity with the number of vertices, suggesting the application of a genetic algorithm for its solution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm shows exponential complexity with the number of vertices, suggesting the application of a genetic algorithm for its solution.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report exponential complexity and the consequent suggestion to consider a genetic algorithm; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting diagrams from the algorithm are qualitatively fully equivalent to manually routed ones.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting diagrams from the algorithm are qualitatively fully equivalent to manually routed ones.
alignments.rationale: The qualitative equivalence claim is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm is successfully applied to several Coupled Cluster approaches and a perturbative CCSD expansion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm is capable of routing non-trivial Goldstone diagrams from given topologies.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed successful application to multiple specific CC approaches, while the publication makes a more general capability statement about routing non-trivial diagrams, which is less specific about those exact applications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for training control policies enables human-robot interactions such as handshakes and hand claps via Deep Reinforcement Learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for training control policies enables human-robot interactions such as handshakes and hand claps via Deep Reinforcement Learning.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A parameterizable multi-objective reward function allows for the learning of various interactions without altering the reward structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A parameterizable multi-objective reward function allows for the learning of various interactions without altering the reward structure.
alignments.rationale: The description of the reward formulation is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parameters of the reward function are estimated from motion capture data of human-human interactions to create policies perceived as natural and human-like.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parameters of the reward function are estimated from motion capture data of human-human interactions to create policies perceived as natural and human-like.
alignments.rationale: The estimation-from-motion-capture claim remains present with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation of the method on hand interactions such as handshake, hand clap, and finger touch demonstrates that the policy produces natural looking motions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of the method on hand interactions such as handshake, hand clap, and finger touch demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A global convergence is emerging around five ethical principles for AI: transparency, justice and fairness, non-maleficence, responsibility, and privacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A global convergence is emerging around five ethical principles for AI: transparency, justice and fairness, non-maleficence, responsibility, and privacy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is substantive divergence in the interpretation, importance, applicability, and implementation of these ethical principles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is substantive divergence in the interpretation, importance, applicability, and implementation of these ethical principles.
alignments.rationale: The finding about substantive divergence is presented identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating guideline-development efforts with substantive ethical analysis and adequate implementation strategies is important.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating guideline-development efforts with substantive ethical analysis and adequate implementation strategies is important.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation to integrate guideline development with ethical analysis and implementation strategies is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed fission clustering (FC) algorithm outperforms other algorithms in speed. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed fission clustering (FC) algorithm outperforms other algorithms in accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fission clustering (FC) utilizes the information of the distance matrix to fissure clustering datasets into subsets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fission clustering (FC) algorithm effectively mines dense families of clusters in datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed reinforcement learning solution rewards the agent for reducing its uncertainty about unobserved portions of its environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed reinforcement learning solution rewards the agent for reducing its uncertainty about unobserved portions of its environment. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The agent is trained to select a short sequence of glimpses to infer the appearance of its full environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The agent is trained to select a short sequence of glimpses to infer the appearance of its full environment. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sidekick policy learning is introduced to address the challenge of sparse rewards by exploiting the asymmetry in observability between training and test time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods developed not only perform the completion task for which they are trained but also generalize to exhibit useful 'look-around' behavior for a range of active perception tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Scattering Power Factorization Framework (SPFF) allows for the direct extraction of $N$ scattering power components and a residue power component for each pixel in PolSAR data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The similarity measure in the SPFF is derived using a geodesic distance between pairs of $4	imes4$ real Kennaugh matrices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The similarity measure in the SPFF is derived using a geodesic distance between pairs of $4	imes 4$ real Kennaugh matrices. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework performs a convex splitting of unity to obtain weights that preserve the dominance of scattering components, differing from standard model-based decomposition schemes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SPFF framework performs a convex splitting of unity to obtain weights that preserve the dominance of the scattering components, differing from standard model-based decomposition schemes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework effectively derives roll-invariant parameters such as scattering-type parameter ($\alpha_{GD}$), helicity parameter ($\tau_{GD}$), and purity parameter ($P_{GD}$). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel method for automatically extracting semi-transparent motion-blurred hands from an RGB image is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The combined solution reduces the required computational resources and time to apply a convolutional neural network (CNN) to extract events from e-sport gameplay videos. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Access to game logs can benefit small e-sport tournament organizers by allowing better visualization of gameplay. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adaptive engagement policies designed in this work are risk-averse, cost-effective, and time-efficient. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adaptive engagement policies designed in this work are risk-averse, cost-effective, and time-efficient. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Reinforcement learning applied to the SMDP achieves a quick and robust convergence of the optimal policy and value under a prudent choice of the learning rate and exploration policy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed video denoising algorithm outperforms other patch-based competitors with significantly lower computing times. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The experiments show that our algorithm compares favorably to other state-of-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
